LAW AND MOTION 301, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT FCA US LLC HAS NOT FILED A RESPONSE TO THE COURT'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY IT SHOULD NOT BE SANCTIONED FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE PARTIES' SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. FCA US LLC IS ORDERED TO PAY $2000 TO PLAINTIFF LORENZO GONZALEZ WITHIN 30 DAYS. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 IS REQUIRED. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

LAW AND MOTION 301,P LAINTIFF LORENZO GONZALEZ'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT. THE UNOPPOSED MOTION TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) DEFENDANT FCA US LLC IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE TERMS OF THE PARTIES' SETTLEMENT. THE COURT ORDERS FCA US LLC TO SHOW CAUSE WHY IT SHOULD NOT BE SANCTIONED $2000, PAYABLE TO PLAINTIFF LORENZO GONZALEZ, FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER. THE COURT SETS THE OSC FOR HEARING ON DECEMBER 4, 2025. FCA SHALL FILE A RESPONSE TO THE OSC NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 25, 2025. IF FCA SHOWS COMPLIANCE WITH THE TERMS OF THE SETTLEMENT IN ITS FILING THE COURT WILL TAKE THE OSC OFF CALENDAR. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-30-2026 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2025 Continued To Feb-10-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: SANCTIONS.
Off Calendar Dec-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: SANCTIONS.

Defendant FCA US LLC has not filed a response to the court's order to show cause why it should not be sanctioned for failure to comply with the parties' settlement agreement. FCA US LLC is ordered to pay $2000 to plaintiff Lorenzo Gonzalez within 30 days.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling. Compliance with CRC 3.1312 is required.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Nov-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LORENZO GONZALEZ'S Motion To Enforce Settlement.

The unopposed motion to enforce the settlement agreement is granted. Defendant FCA US LLC is ordered to comply with the terms of the parties' settlement. The court orders FCA US LLC to show cause why it should not be sanctioned $2000, payable to plaintiff Lorenzo Gonzalez, for failure to comply with this order. The court sets the OSC for hearing on December 4, 2025. FCA shall file a response to the OSC no later than November 25, 2025. If FCA shows compliance with the terms of the settlement in its filing the court will take the OSC off calendar.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-17-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-12-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of Matters Admitted In Plaintiff'S Requests For Admissions (Set One)
Off Calendar May-21-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LORENZO GONZALEZ'S Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of Matters Admitted In Plaintiff'S Requests For Admissions (Set One)

The motion is moot since responses were served after the filing of this motion. However, the responses were late and all objections have been waived. Defendants' responses contain a litany of objections. To retain those objections, Defendants need to file a motion under the various discovery statutes for relief from waiver. Defendants are directed to file any such motion by June 7, 2024. If such motion is filed and granted, the parties shall meet and confer on the responses before another motion is filed. If the motion is denied, Defendants must provide complete responses without objections. The Court would have awarded sanctions, but none were requested.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem David Mcdonald at davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
